title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the overflow-safe reference-counted graph contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for overflow-safe reference-counted graph after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retain Release Invariant · changed case · Review Retain Release Invariant
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 9541. Produce bounded C evidence to model strong counts and expose a cycle that cannot reach zero; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
